title: Ten Movies to Stream, Best Picture Power Rankings, and an Ask Amanda Mailbag
format: Sean and Amanda pick through the PGA, WGA, and Ace Eddie nominations, lock in Amanda's final Best Picture ten, clear out a backlog of streaming recommendations, and close with an Ask Amanda mailbag before she heads out on leave.

films.title: Being the Ricardos
films.note: The Ace Eddie nominee they agree is 'the ultimate SAG movie,' built for guild recognition even if neither is a fan.

films.title: Belfast
films.note: Kenneth Branagh's film was ineligible for the WGA on a technicality, but they assume it lands in original screenplay anyway.

films.title: CODA
films.note: Premiered at Sundance in January 2021; Sean calls the Apple TV+ release one of the least-seen movies ever nominated for Best Picture.

films.title: Don't Look Up
films.note: Sean's pick for the contender in the most comfortable position: a streaming event movie from a filmmaker (Adam McKay) on his third straight awards-season run.

films.title: Dune
films.note: Denis Villeneuve's film had a 30-day HBO Max window and then vanished; they debate whether that helps or hurts it with Oscar voters.

films.title: King Richard
films.note: Also caught in the day-and-date HBO Max window with Dune, now available only as a pricier VOD rental.

films.title: Licorice Pizza
films.note: Called a 'lock' after picking up a Best Edited Feature (Comedy) Ace Eddie nomination.

films.title: The Power of the Dog
films.note: Jane Campion's film was ineligible for the WGA; they expect it in adapted screenplay regardless.

films.title: Tick, Tick... Boom!
films.note: Andrew Garfield stars in Lin-Manuel Miranda's directorial debut; Amanda argues it's a desperate grab at 'Hamilton magic dust' and the most vulnerable of the ten PGA nominees.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: Steven Spielberg and Tony Kushner's remake of a film that already won Best Picture; strong on paper, but nobody's actually going to see it in theaters.

films.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
films.note: Missed the PGA's top 10 (the group that historically rewards a blockbuster), which Sean reads as a bad sign for its Best Picture chances.

films.title: No Time to Die
films.note: Also shut out of the PGA nominations.

films.title: Drive My Car
films.note: The 'critical darling,' but ineligible for the WGA; neither host believes it actually cracks the Best Picture ten.

films.title: The Lost Daughter
films.note: Maggie Gyllenhaal's film was also WGA-ineligible; Amanda's pick to replace Tick, Tick... Boom! in the final ten.

films.title: The Tragedy of Macbeth
films.note: Named as one of three smaller films that could still slide into a PGA snub's spot.

films.title: House of Gucci
films.note: Sean eulogizes it ('pour out a gigantic magnum of Barolo') after it missed the PGA nominations entirely.

films.title: Nightmare Alley
films.note: Guillermo del Toro's film got a late awards push (Martin Scorsese wrote an op-ed praising it) but only mustered the one WGA adapted-screenplay nomination.

films.title: The French Dispatch
films.note: Wes Anderson's film picked up a WGA original screenplay nod and an Ace Eddie comedy-editing nomination.

films.title: Cruella
films.note: The surprise Ace Eddie comedy-editing nominee; neither host is sure why.

films.title: The Lost Daughter
films.note: Amanda swaps this in for Tick, Tick... Boom!, the one change she makes from the PGA lineup.

segments.heading: Ten Movies to Stream
segments.note: not a lot in theaters right now, so here's what to watch at home

films.title: A Hero
films.note: Asghar Farhadi's Amazon Prime drama about a debtor's-prison inmate who finds a bag of money; Sean calls it one of the best movies of the last twelve months.

films.title: Bergman Island
films.note: Mia Hansen-Løve's film, on Hulu, about two screenwriters (Tim Roth and Vicky Krieps) staying on Bergman's Fårö; Amanda's 'strong recommend.'

films.title: The Worst Person in the World
films.note: Coming to theaters February 4th; Anders Danielsen Lie, who also appears in Bergman Island, gets singled out for both performances.

films.title: Swan Song
films.note: Benjamin Cleary's Apple TV+ film has Mahershala Ali playing both an original and a clone of himself after a terminal diagnosis.

films.title: I'm Your Man
films.note: Maria Schrader's German film, on Hulu, with Dan Stevens as a pleasure robot and Maren Eggert as the woman testing a relationship with him.

films.title: The Last Duel
films.note: Back on HBO Max via the old Fox/Disney output deal despite never having been a Warner release; Ridley Scott's Matt Damon/Ben Affleck/Jodie Comer medieval trial drama.

films.title: Malignant
films.note: James Wan's HBO Max horror movie, back after its 30-day window; Sean calls it a Brian De Palma homage.

films.title: Munich: The Edge of War
films.note: Amanda's pick, with Jeremy Irons as Neville Chamberlain; she and Sean both find its take on Chamberlain surprisingly generous.

films.title: The House
films.note: A stop-motion anthology on Netflix with multiple directors.

films.title: The Fallout
films.note: Directed by Megan Park, about the aftermath of a school shooting; premiered at SXSW 2021.

films.title: The Last Thing Mary Saw
films.note: A period horror film on Shudder that Sean watched at 1 a.m.

films.title: Sundown
films.note: Michel Franco's film, in theaters, with Tim Roth as a man who abandons his family's return trip home from Acapulco.

segments.heading: Ask Amanda Mailbag
segments.note: listener questions before Amanda heads out on leave

films.title: The Goldfinch
films.note: Amanda's pick for a Donna Tartt adaptation redo, with Joe Wright directing.

films.title: Pride & Prejudice
films.note: Cited as one of Joe Wright's literary adaptations Amanda loves.

films.title: Atonement
films.note: Also cited as a Joe Wright adaptation; Amanda separately recommends the Ian McEwan novel it's based on.

films.title: Anna Karenina
films.note: The third Joe Wright literary adaptation Amanda name-checks.

films.title: Cyrano
films.note: Amanda's dig at 'a weird musical of an old book' while wishing for more straight adaptations.

films.title: Spencer
films.note: Amanda's dream-biopic answer starts with 'Spencer wasn't it for me,' before she pitches an Agatha Christie biopic instead.

films.title: The American President
films.note: Amanda and her dad saw Aaron Sorkin's film in theaters at least four times in 1995.

films.title: Top Five
films.note: One of the films Amanda's dad got swept up in wondering if it could win Best Picture.

films.title: Morning Glory
films.note: Another of her dad's Best Picture false alarms.

films.title: Fifty Shades of Grey
films.note: Amanda's favorite-theater-experience answer: an opening-weekend Valentine's Day screening in Brooklyn.

films.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
films.note: A blizzard screening with Zach that Amanda calls one of her favorite movie theater memories.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: A Chinese Theatre premiere Amanda and Zach attended with assigned seats apart.

films.title: Paddington
films.note: Referenced collectively with its sequel as Andy Greenwald's favorite movies to bring up unprompted.

films.title: Singin' in the Rain
films.note: Amanda's answer for the first movie her child will see, ideally by age six or seven.

films.title: Bringing Up Baby
films.note: Amanda's pick for around age nine or ten, cited for its dog, leopard, and 'a dinosaur.'

films.title: The Philadelphia Story
films.note: Zach's suggestion that Amanda vetoes as too old a watch for a kid.

films.title: Cool Hand Luke
films.note: One of two films Zach's dad showed him before age ten.

films.title: Halloween
films.note: The other early Zach-as-a-kid watch, despite Zach not otherwise being a horror fan.

films.title: Star Wars
films.note: Zach's earliest movie-theater memory, watched as a small child.

films.title: The Sound of Music
films.note: Amanda's own family holiday tradition she plans to pass down.

films.title: Broadcast News
films.note: Amanda quotes the film answering how it feels to always be right: 'it's awful.'

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: Referenced via the movie-draft-rules blowup: Amanda's 'fealty to Maverick' when Sean bent the rules on a past auction.

films.title: Heat
films.note: The film a listener asks whether Amanda will eventually let her kid watch, courtesy of Chris Ryan.

The French Dispatch.title: The French Dispatch of the Liberty, Kansas Evening Sun

The Worst Person in the World.title: The Worst Person in the World

Borat Subsequent Moviefilm.title: Borat Subsequent Moviefilm: Delivery of Prodigious Bribe to American Regime for Make Benefit Once Glorious Nation of Kazakhstan
